danger_signals.visual_form: Objects ejected from character bodies during combat (dumbbells, hearts, mallet)
danger_signals.meaning: Visual indicator of damage taken and character distress

danger_signals.visual_form: Uppercuts launching opponent high into air
danger_signals.meaning: Successful hit landing; opponent vulnerability state

danger_signals.visual_form: Over-the-top cartoonish attacks (arm transforming into blade, fists catching fire)
danger_signals.meaning: High-damage attack incoming; imminent danger to opponent

safety_signals.visual_form: Character grounded in neutral position in ring
safety_signals.meaning: Ready state; opponent not currently attacking

safety_signals.visual_form: Announcer commentary and ring state changes between rounds

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: WWF branding on ring and announcers table
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Announcers table with Vince McMahon and Jerry Lawler; Howard Finkel at entrance; WrestleMania event signage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tournament bracket structure; single matches progress through four 1v1 matches, two 2v1 handicap matches, one 3v1 handicap match
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponent difficulty and number scales progressively

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Escalating match structure displayed in UI; final gauntlet mode triggered
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players face increasing opponent counts (3v1 gauntlet) with escalating AI aggression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Two-player cooperative setup; paired opponent groups in ring simultaneously
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponents fight in coordinated pairs; players must manage two-on-two dynamics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Title screen theme; announcer Howard Finkel welcoming to WrestleMania; entrance music from circa-1994 WWF era
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Characters perform entrance animations and signature move sets reflective of WWF 1994-1995 roster characterization

environmental_storytelling.example: Lex Luger bleeds dumbbells; Bret Hart bleeds valentine hearts; Doink bleeds mallets - each object represents character gimmick and personality
Each wrestler's circa-1994 WWF entrance theme plays on selection and entrance, reinforcing their individual identity and push during that era
Vince McMahon and Jerry 'The King' Lawler visibly present at announcers table throughout match, providing real-time narrative framing of wrestler conflict
Razor Ramon's arm morphing into blade, Bam Bam Bigelow's fists catching fire - exaggerated physical transformations communicate wrestler personas through hyperrealistic mechanics
Handicap match structure (2v1, 3v1) visually communicates power imbalance and challenge escalation through opponent count on-screen

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Stance neutral in ring; bleeds valentine hearts when damaged
Performs supernatural-themed attacks; Western Mortician era characterization from 1991-1994 theme music
Uses entrance theme 'Sexy Boy' (older Sherri Martel version); characterized by flashy offense
Arm transforms into blade during special moves; aggressive strike-heavy offense
Fists catch on fire during special attacks; power-based character archetype
Large heavyweight character; present in arcade/Genesis/32X versions; omitted in SNES port due to cartridge constraints
Pulls mallet from thin air during attacks; comedic gimmick character with cartoonish physics
Bleeds dumbbells when damaged; reflects muscular strongman gimmick
Character fully realized in arcade version; Midway confirmed as hidden character, though developer confirmed it was not fully completed

notes: This arcade fighting game uses WWF wrestler personality manifestation rather than deep faction systems. World-state is communicated through tournament structure (single-elimination vs. handicap match progression) and character-specific visual effects tied to wrestling gimmicks. The SNES port degradation (character roster reduction to 6 from 8, 3 on-screen max vs 4) represents technical constraint rather than narrative world-state change. Digitized graphics and exaggerated Mortal Kombat-influenced physics create a wrestling-themed fighting game presentation rather than simulation. Real-world WWE/WWF political territory divisions are absent; focus is on individual wrestler characterization and match hierarchy.
